title: The Sealed Gate
subtitle: Thresholds & Containment

lore.description: The Sealed Gate stands where realities meet, embodying the sacred power of thresholds, boundaries, and containment. Its followers are wardens, exorcists, and philosophers of separation who believe some things must be kept apart for the world to function. The Gate manifests as an impassive judge whose shifting sigils — binding runes, exclusion marks, or acceptance glyphs — determine what may cross.
lore.followers: Among the peoples of Fate's Edge, the Sealed Gate is known as the patron of abjurists, exorcists, and sentinels. In Aeler, the Gate is called the Stone Seal, invoked by True-Masons who carve permanent wards into mountain passes. In Thepyrgos, the Gate is the Unbreakable Lock, whose sigils are painted on every archive door that holds forbidden texts. In the Mistlands, the Gate is the Bell-Line Keeper, the force that makes the bell-wards hold against the Direwood. Wherever something must be kept in or kept out, the Sealed Gate's servants are there — silent, patient, and absolute.
lore.quote: You define what passes and what remains. Every boundary remembers your judgment.

domain_focus: Thresholds: doors, portals, borders between realms
Containment: wards, bindings, imprisonment of dangerous forces
Exclusion: banishment, quarantine, protection through separation
Permission: safe passage, authorized access, controlled crossing

thiasos.description: A Runekeeper sworn to the Sealed Gate does not bind a living creature — they bind a threshold given will. Their Thiasos is a small object imbued with boundary-sense: a bronze key that fits no lock yet trembles when a boundary is near, a brass bell that rings only when a ward is about to break, or a small iron gate that stands on the Runekeeper's desk and opens only when a lie is spoken.
thiasos.care: This object requires no food, but it must be anointed with oil and placed on a threshold at dawn each day. If neglected for a week, the Thiasos becomes Compromised (it emits a low hum that imposes –1 die on Stealth rolls for its bearer).

codex.description: The Codex of the Sealed Gate is never a simple book. It may be a leather strap carved with runes that tighten when a ward is violated, a set of iron rings linked in a chain, or the inside of a lock.
codex.upkeep: Because boundaries are physical, the Codex requires upkeep: the Runekeeper must spend an hour each week testing each seal (touching, ringing, or whispering to it), or the Rites stored there become uncertain (treat as Compromised until restored).

patrons_gift.name: Gate's Blessing
patrons_gift.description: Once per scene as an action, a Runekeeper of the Sealed Gate may touch a held item (weapon, shield, or tool) and speak a word of exclusion.
patrons_gift.effect: For the rest of the scene, the item grants +1 die to Defend or Resist rolls, and the first time each scene the bearer would be moved against their will (shoved, pulled, or teleported), they may ignore it.
patrons_gift.cost: After the scene, the user marks +1 Obligation to the Sealed Gate.

rites.name: Rite of the Sealed Threshold
rites.materials: Chalk, iron dust, or sanctified cord.
rites.effect: Mark a doorway or passage as sealed. Unauthorized crossing tests Spirit+Resolve (DV 3) or suffers a Position penalty. Creates a 4-segment Ward Integrity timer.
rites.push_it: The barrier becomes a visible shimmer, imposing –1 die on attempts to cross it. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue.

rites.name: Rite of the Key's Rebuke
rites.materials: A gesture of denial or a barrier symbol.
rites.effect: The target must test Body+Resolve (DV 3) or be pushed back from the threshold. On success, create a Rebuke Echo token granting +1 die to the next boundary defense.
rites.push_it: The rebuke lingers — the target cannot cross this threshold for the rest of the scene. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue.

rites.name: Rite of the Circle of Denial
rites.materials: A salt ring, iron filings, or boundary stones.
rites.effect: Create a protective circle. Supernatural beings test against the caster's Spirit+Arcana (DV 4) to cross. Mortals suffer –2 dice to force entry. Creates a 6-segment Circle Integrity timer.
rites.push_it: The circle becomes a two-way barrier (no one can cross). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue.

rites.name: Rite of the Writ of Passage
rites.effect: Designate an authorized passage route. Allies gain Position +1 when using the route. Unauthorized intruders suffer –1 die to movement or stealth. Creates an 8-segment Writ Authority timer.
rites.push_it: Extend authorization to additional allies; mark +1 Obligation. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue.

rites.name: Rite of the Banishment Knot
rites.materials: A knotted cord sealed with a gate-sigil.
rites.effect: The target supernatural entity tests against the caster's Spirit+Resolve (DV = entity Cap). On success, the entity is forced to immediately depart the scene. A failed attempt creates a 4-segment Banishment Backlash timer.
rites.push_it: The banishment lasts until the next moon phase. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue.

rites.name: Rite of the Consecrated Barrier
rites.materials: Relics from three traditions, boundary markers, a trespasser's acknowledgment.
rites.effect: Consecrate an area against all unauthorized passage. The tests required scale with the intrusion attempt's severity. A 10-segment Sacred Boundary timer tracks the barrier's strength.
rites.push_it: Make the barrier semi-permanent (lasts one season); start a Maintenance [6] timer. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue.

cantors.name: The Cantor's Song
cantors.description: A Cantor of the Sealed Gate sings in locks and wards — melodies that are precise, repetitive, and final, like the click of a deadbolt or the thud of a bar being set. Their voice is used to seal a door with a sung phrase, to recite a litany of exclusion that pushes a spirit back across the threshold, or to sing a sleeping guard into dreaming of a wall that never ends. The Gate's Cantors are often found in prisons, asylums, and the gatehouses of cities, their songs keeping the unwanted out.

cult.name: The Cult of the Unbroken Seal
cult.description: The Gate's cults meet in places where boundaries are most needed: at city gates, on bridges, in the guardrooms of tombs, and before the sealed doors of forgotten vaults. The Unbroken Seal is a fellowship of wardens, gate-keepers, exorcists, and sentinels who believe that a well-kept boundary is the foundation of civilization. A ritual of the Cult involves each member renewing a vow to guard a specific threshold — a door, a river, a mountain pass — and marking it with a fresh sigil. Their creed is carved into a single stone that is never moved: 'What is sealed stays sealed. What stays sealed protects the living.' Outsiders are welcome to witness the sealing, but they must leave a token of their own boundary — a key, a lock of hair, a written promise.

witchcraft.description: A witch who walks with the Sealed Gate is a ward-keeper, an exorcist archivist, or a boundary-scholar — one who uses seals, litanies, and physical tokens to enforce separation. Their magic is not forged but written or sung: sealing wax mixed with iron filings, a bell that rings only when a threshold is violated, or a bound notebook of exclusion rites whose pages turn black when a spirit tries to cross. In Aeler, a True-Mason might carve a 'ward stone' into a lintel, but a hedge-witch of the Gate might daub a lignite sigil on a doorframe while reciting the Ninth Clause of Stillness. In Viterra, a village exorcist might carry a small iron seal to stamp the corners of a haunted room.

iron_seal.name: The Seal of the Unbroken Gate
iron_seal.description: A small iron seal on a chain, cool to the touch, its face engraved with a closed door. Once per session, the witch may press the seal into wax (or directly onto a threshold while speaking a word of denial). For the next scene, any creature attempting to cross that threshold with hostile intent must make a Spirit+Resolve test (DV 3) or be turned back. The seal may also be used to 'witness' a lock: touching it to a mundane lock imposes +1 DV on any attempt to pick it until the next dawn. The seal's warmth tells the witch whether any binding placed with it still holds.

hedge_gifts.name: Sealed Lips
hedge_gifts.description: Once per scene, the witch may touch a person's lips (or a letter's seal) and whisper a litany of the Gate. For the next hour, that person cannot speak a specific secret or name (chosen by the witch). If they try, their voice fails — they cough, stutter, or simply cannot form the words. This effect can be removed by the witch or by a Resolve test (DV 4) made once per day.

corruption.benefit: Threshold Sense: +1 die to detect weak points, unauthorized passage, or boundary violations.
corruption.cost: Paranoid Vigilance: Must repeatedly check seals; –1 die to trust-based actions.

corruption.benefit: Ward Resonance: Once/scene, treat a failed protection roll as a success (mark 1 SB).
corruption.cost: Isolation Tendency: 1 Fatigue in unsecured or open spaces.

corruption.benefit: Boundary Mastery: +2 dice to ward, barrier, or containment magic.
corruption.cost: Compulsive Sealing: Must secure any vulnerability noticed.

corruption.benefit: Absolute Barrier: Once/session, create an impenetrable barrier (size of a door) for one exchange.
corruption.cost: Prison Mindset: –1 die to freedom or escape actions.

corruption.benefit: Gate Sovereign: Once/session, banish a major threat with a single test (Spirit+Resolve vs Cap+1).
corruption.cost: Boundary Obsession: –1 die to non-containment actions.

corruption.benefit: Threshold Incarnate: For one scene, become a living ward. Allies within Near gain +1 Armor; any creature attempting to pass a threshold you guard must test Spirit+Resolve (DV 5) or be turned back.
corruption.cost: Ultimate Containment Risk: Mark +3 Obligation. You cannot leave the warded area until the scene ends, and you suffer 1 Fatigue.

playstyle_notes.description: The Sealed Gate patron excels in defensive, control-oriented play. Followers shape battlefields through strategic boundary placement and become invaluable when dealing with supernatural intrusions or securing locations. The corruption progression naturally leads characters toward becoming immovable guardians at the cost of flexibility and freedom. Ideal for abjurists, exorcists, sentinels, and any character whose duty is to keep things apart.
emphasizes: Thresholds: doors, portals, borders between realms
Containment: wards, bindings, imprisonment of dangerous forces
Exclusion: banishment, quarantine, protection through separation
Permission: safe passage, authorized access, controlled crossing
Immovable Guardian: the follower becomes a wall given will

monastic_tradition.name: Threshold Monk (Sealed Gate)
monastic_tradition.quote: You define what passes and what remains.
monastic_tradition.prerequisites: Spirit 3+, Wits 2+, Rite of the Sealed Threshold or Cantor of the Sealed Gate.
monastic_tradition.debt_resistant_frame: A threshold does not owe — it defines. Monks of the Threshold Monk tradition gain +1 die to resist any magical debt or obligation that would force them to cross a boundary they do not wish to cross. If they succeed, the debtor finds the way inexplicably barred (a door will not open, a contract will not bind).

techniques.name: Iron Rebuke
techniques.effect: When an enemy enters your Close range, make a free unarmed strike. On a hit, they cannot move closer this exchange.

techniques.name: Seal the Threshold
techniques.effect: Mark a doorway or passage as sealed. Unauthorized crossing tests Spirit+Resolve (DV 3) or suffers a Position penalty. Create a 4-segment Ward Integrity timer.

techniques.name: Key's Rebuke
techniques.effect: Force target to test Body+Resolve (DV 3) or be pushed back from a threshold you guard.

techniques.name: Circle of Denial
techniques.effect: Create a protective circle (salt or chalk). Supernatural beings test against your Spirit+Arcana (DV 4) to enter. Mortals suffer –2 dice to force entry. Create a 6-segment Circle Integrity timer.

techniques.name: Sanctuary Step
techniques.effect: When you would be moved against your will (shoved, pulled, teleported), you may ignore it.

techniques.name: Banishment Knot
techniques.effect: Force a supernatural entity to test Spirit+Resolve (DV = entity Cap) or depart the scene immediately.

master_technique.name: Threshold Incarnate
master_technique.description: Once per session, become a living ward for one scene. Allies within Near gain +1 Armor; any creature attempting to pass a threshold you guard must test Spirit+Resolve (DV 5) or be turned back. After the scene, you gain a permanent Debt Scar (a door-shaped mark on your palm) — but you may ignore one debt or obligation that would force you to abandon your guard (the creditor finds themselves facing a wall where once there was a door).

sample_adventure.title: The Unsealed Vault
sample_adventure.description: A vault beneath the Law Quarter of Valora has been breached. The seals were not broken from the outside — they were opened from within. Something that was imprisoned is now walking the city, wearing the face of the warden who sealed it. The party must track the escapee, learn what it truly is, and decide whether to seal it again or let it walk free. The Sealed Gate's servants are divided: some believe in redemption, others in containment. The decision will shape the city's future.
sample_adventure.quote: Some doors are sealed for a reason. The Gate does not judge the prisoner — it only keeps the boundary. But when the boundary breaks, the Gate must decide: to seal again, or to let pass what should have stayed contained.
